rows.title: Conservation, Collection and Distribution of Spring Water to Provide Irrigation Facility to the High Hill Smallholder Farmers to the Collective Apple Farm
rows.description: The main objective of the project is to increase income of the high hill smallholder farmers (the target beneficiaries) by increasing productivity of apple and vegetables through the provision of irrigation water supply all-round the year.

rows.title: Assessing the impact of the global crisis on the agriculture and food security situation in Nepal
rows.description: The major objective of this joint programme is to assess the impact of the global crisis on Nepal?s food system, food security, household level agriculture and income, livestock production, food and essential commodity prices and market functionality, and to explore the impact of the crisis on Nepal?s progress towards achieving SDG targets (mainly SDG 2). The differential impact the crisis has on urban and rural households and chronically vulnerable groups will also be assessed and analyzed. WFP and FAO will build on existing tools and systems for food security monitoring and emergency impact assessments (a combination of household surveys, market assessments and desk reviews as well as consultations with relevant stakeholders), with the evidence generated shared with the Government, development and humanitarian partners and other stakeholders to inform the design of potential response plans and strategic interventions by humanitarian and development agencies, as well as to inform Government policies, plans and programmes.

rows.title: Accelerating Progress towards Rural Women`s Economic Empowerment (JP RWEE) Phase II
rows.description: The proposed programme will work towards strengthening the economic capacity and resilience of rural women in order to address the structural barriers that preventing their social and economic inclusion, whilst also responding to the negative effects of COVID-19 and  climate change. The intervention aims to create an enabling environment for employability, productivity gains, household income and gender-responsive policies to secure rural women?s livelihood, rights and resilience.

rows.title: MyRight CSO strategi 2025-2028
rows.description:
Programmets övergripande syfte är att: partnerorganisationer är en aktiv del av civilsamhället, arbetar effektivt för att hålla ansvarsbärare och utvecklingsaktörer ansvariga för förverkligandet av mänskliga rättigheter för människor med funktionsnedsättning i enlighet med Konventionen om rättigheter för personer med funktionsnedsättning (KRPF) och Globala målen (framförallt mål 1, 3, 4, 5, 8, 10 och 16)

Programmet kommer bidra till det syftet genom tre resultat:

1. Stärkande av individers egenmakt: PO:er har förbättrad kapacitet och verktyg att stärka personer med funktionsnedsättning i att hantera utmaningar, öka sitt självbestämmande och göra sina röster hörda, samt att öka medvetenheten hos deras familjer och i det lokala samhället om rättigheterna för PwD. 

2. Stärkande av organisatorisk kapacitet: PO:er har förbättrat sina organisatoriska strukturer, ledningssystem, miljöengagemang, kompetens och resursmobilisering för att bli mer finansiellt självständiga, demokratiska, transparenta, ansvariga, genus- och miljömedvetna, effektiva och relevanta.  

3.  Stärkande av påverkansarbete och policydialog: PO:er har utvecklat och använt sina påverkans- och nätverksförmågor vilket möjliggör att de kan utkräva ansvar från beslutsfattare för deras åtaganden kring rättigheterna för PwD.

rows.description:
Insatsens mål

Programmets övergripande mål och syfte är att säkerställa att barns rättigheter realiseras och respekteras. Problemformuleringen utgår från hur barns rättigheter utsätts för överlappande och ökande utmaningar kopplat till klimatförändringar, konflikter och kvinnors och flickors inskränkta rättigheter. Över en miljard barn världen över saknar tillgång till grundläggande rättigheter såsom utbildning, hälsa och mat. 

Förändringsteorin utgår ifrån att ett starkt civilsamhälle är en av de mest avgörande aktörerna för att utkräva att barns rättigheter uppfylls, att stater hålls ansvariga och att barn och deras samhällen stärks. Det övergripande målets har formulerats på engelska och återges därför ordagrant nedan: 

Civil society actors, including children, claim civic space and have the capacity to hold duty-bearers accountable for the equitable fulfilment of childrens rights

Den nedbrutna förändringsteorin är att om civilsamhällesaktörer har finansiering, samarbeten, evidensbaserade metoder och nödvändig kapacitet; får de bättre möjlighet övervaka efterlevnaden och påverka politik för barns rättigheter; och då nås programmålet. Målet ska nås genom två underliggande resultat/metoder, även de återgivna på engelska: 

1. Monitor and Report: Civil society actors, including children, have increased capacity and opportunities to monitor, report and follow-up on duty-bearers commitments to child rights.

Under det här resultatområdet ingår till exempel bevakning och rapportering till processer som barnkonventionen, Universal Periodic Review, Voluntary National Reviews och liknande regionala processer. Lokala forskningsinitiativ ingår också under detta område. Varje land kommer identifiera sina egna strategier och resultat utifrån sin specifika kontext men några exempel är redan identifierade inom programmet.

- Uppföljningen av policyer, lagar och budgetering för barn och deras rättigheter i en konfliktkontext kommer följas upp på nationell och regional nivå i Ukraina. 

- I Tanzania planeras upprättandet av en Barnrättsakademi där utbildning och resurser ska finnas tillgängliga för barn och partnerorganisationer med syftet att ge de metoder och ett samlat förhållningsätt för att följa upp Tanzanias barnrättsåtaganden. 

- Informella barnrättsaktörer och grupper ska få stöd att samla in evidens och rapportera prioriterade områden och hålla beslutsfattare ansvariga i Kambodja. 

- I Kosovo kommer marginaliserade barn få stöd att utkräva ansvar vad gäller utbildning, klimatfrågor och jämställdhet med hjälp av digitala plattformar, samhällsrevisioner och lokala scorecards. 

2. Influence: Civil society actors, including children, have strengthened capacity and opportunities to influence policies, laws, budgets and services to advance the rights of girls and boys in all their diversities.

Under det här målområdet kommer kapacitetsstärkande åtgärder för partnerorganisationer och barn genomföras, med syftet att stärka dem att säkra nationell till lokal styrning som säkrar barns tillgång till rättigheter, skydd och samhällstjänster. Varje land kommer identifiera sina egna strategier och resultat utifrån sin specifika kontext men några exempel är redan planerade.

- I Etiopien kommer partnerorganisationer stöttas att utkräva inkluderande utbildning, förskoleutbildning, och skyddsnät för barn i utsatta situationer såsom konflikt, genusbaserat våld eller internflyktingar.

- Programmet i Nepal kommer fortsätta verka genom det av partner nyetablerade parlamentariska forumet för barnrättsfrågor. I forumet kommer flickor, barn från marginaliserade grupper (Daliter, LGBTIQ, barn med funktionsnedsättningar) ges utrymme att utkräva deras rättigheter i policyer och offentliga budgetar. 

- I Elfenbenskusten kommer stöd gå till civilsamhällets deltagande i reformkommittéer med syfte att skapa harmoniserade lagar kring barnaga, genusbaserat våld och integrering av miljö- och klimatfrågor i barnrättslagar. 

- Att barn och barnrätt prioriteras i den nationella utvecklingsagendan, vad gäller övergrepp mot barn, jämställdhet och barns ökade resiliens i klimatkatastrofer kommer bli centralt i programmet i Zambia.

rows.title: Master's Degree Program in Water Resources Management(2025)
rows.description: Offer courses structured around three elective options: Integrated Water Resources Management, Water Quality and Aquatic Ecosystem Management, and Water Disaster Management, each with corresponding curricula. Our curriculum focuses on developing capabilities for systematic water resource development, including projects like water resource informatization and the formulation of water resource management plans and long-term master plans. Additionally, we provide courses aimed at improving water quality issues in developing countries and conserving water ecosystems for sustainable development. We also offer curricula focused on understanding and researching appropriate response technologies for medium to long-term planning and addressing current issues related to water-related disasters. Key courses include  Water Action Plan, which provides a fundamental understanding of execution plans for water resources management and facilitates the development of water.

rows.title: Avoidable Blindness Initiative
rows.description: The Australian Government is committed to ensuring that the benefits of development reach those who are most excluded and, as such, people with a disability have been identified as a priority for Australia's aid program.  This initiative specifically addresses the government's commitment to developing an avoidable blindness program. Funds will support a range of activities including: $15 million over 2 years in partnership with Vision 2020 Australia Global Consortium to expand existing eye and vision care in Asia and the Pacific; and $9.8 million over 3 years to strengthen training and capacity of eye health workers in Pacific, East Timor and PNG. rows.title: Nepal Kosi River Flood Relief 2008 - UNICEF
rows.description: On 18 August the eastern embankment of the Kosi Riverin south-easten Nepal collapsed resulting in major flooding in Nepal and in Bihar in north-east India. Estimates are that 70,000 people are displaced and are in need of shelter and assistance. Nepal is also providing relief for more than 10,000 displaced people from Bihar who have crossed into Nepal seeking assistance. The humanitarian repsonse has been well managed and coordinated by the Government of Nepal at both the central and local level. UNICEF has requested US$1.8 million to provide shelter, safe water, sanitation facilities, hygiene kits, education and child protection.

rows.description: There are currently 33,404 Early Childhood Development centres (ECDCs) in Nepal, open to children between 3-5 years of age, which aim to increase enrolment and retention of children in primary schools. This pilot project will provide training to visually impaired children in daily living skills and pre-Braille activities to encourage ECDCs to enrol them, something which is currently not happening. The Department for Education in Nepal is keen to take this approach, if successful, to scale as a part of an Education for All Children with Visual Impairment (EFAVI) campaign to meet targets of the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs).

rows.title: South Asia Water Initiative
rows.description: The South Asia Water Initiative (SAWI) aims to promote regional cooperation in the sustainable management of the water resources of the Himalayan rivers to address the challenges of development and climate change.  The program will be structured around five key focus areas: (1) Ganges Basin, (2) Brahmaputra Basin, (3) Indus Basin, (4) Sundarbans Landscape, and (5) Regional Cooperation. To promote more effective and cooperative water resources management, SAWI will facilitate multi-stakeholder dialogue and policy deliberation; enhance knowledge and capacity on issues of regional, and in particular trans-boundary water resources management and climate adaptation; and promote cooperative actions such as policy reforms, institutional development, and investment in water resources management. The total value of this multi-country initiative is $9.0 million over 5 years, starting 2008-09.

rows.title: 2008-09 country allocation of IRF
rows.description:
Funding through the IRF is targeted in a strategic way to address the macro-context issues within particular countries and regions where protracted displacement and refugee situations feature as co-indicators and/or drivers of under-development.   

The 2008-09 budget provides for the continuation of a $15 million allocation to the IRF.  In accordance with a 19 Jan Ministerial Submission the IRF will allocate in a strategic way through funding to country operations of key multilaterals or through strategic UN appeals.

rows.title: Support for Mine Detectors to UN Nepal
rows.description:
In kind support of 10 mine detectors through UNOPS.

Initiative end date changed to meet reporting requirements by UNOPS.

rows.title: Civil Society Water, Sanitation and Hygiene Fund
rows.description: The Water, Sanitation and Hygiene Civil Society Fund provides funding to civil society organisations to engage in water, sanitation and hygiene projects in developing countries in Asia, the Pacific and Africa over the duration of the Water and Sanitation Initiative.  This is a multi-country initiative with a total value of $21.7 million over 3 years, starting 2009-10.

rows.title: 2008-2009 UNDAC, INSARAG and APHP Activities
rows.description: The UN Disaster Assessment and Coordination (UNDAC), the International Urban Search and Rescue Advisory Group (INSARAG) and the Asia-Pacific Humanitarian Partnership (APHP) are international mechanisms that AusAID supports to responds to International emergencies. Funding under this initiatives addresses the preparedness training for INSARAG and APHP as well support the Australian's UNDAC deployment account, to retain the capability to deploy Australian UNDAC members on emergency response missions when needed. The total value of this initiative is $0.1 million over 2 years, starting 2008-09.

rows.title: International Seminar Support Scheme Phase II
rows.description: The International Seminar Support Scheme (ISSS) is a small grant scheme that funds developing country participants attendance at international development-oriented seminars/conferences in Australia and overseas.    The scheme helps to develop knowledge and technical expertise in developing countries, and builds linkages between the government, academic and community sectors in Australia and our partner developing countries.    ISSS is a discretionary scheme. Funds are limited and competition for them is strong. Applications need to provide a convincing case for support, with clear objectives and details of how they will be achieved. Successful applications must demonstrate tangible outcomes and practical contributions to the economic development/social advancement of developing countries. The total value of this multi-country initiative is $4.2 million over 3 years.

rows.title: Nepal Health Sector Program SWAp(IHP) Phase 1
rows.description: Funding under this initiative is supporting the Government of Nepal's Health Sector Program (NHSP) SWAp, in particular Nepal Health Sector Program Implementation Plan Phase 1 (NHSP IP 1).  This initiative provides AusAID funding as a pooled donor supporting the NHSP SWAp which has been led by the Government of Nepal (GoN) since 2004. The NHSP SWAp supports the GoN's goal of "improving the health status of the Nepalese population through increased utilization of essential health services...". The NHSP aligns with Australian Government priorities in the health sector. Nepal was one of seven countries selected as International Health Partnership (IHP) pilot countries. The partnership aims to accelerate progress towards health related Millennium Development Goals (MDG) through improved co-ordination of donor and country activities based around national health plans. The total value of this initiative is $6.7 million over 3 years, starting 2008-09.

rows.title: Water and Sanitation Initiative Global Programming
rows.description: Water and Sanitation Initiative (WSI) Global Programming is an initiative to fund global and regional water, sanitation and hygiene programs delivered through multilateral partners. The purpose of the funding was to widen the geographical focus and impact of water, sanitation and hygiene programs; complement activities funded bilaterally; and to work with multilateral partners to improve aid effectiveness, build partner capacity and enhance knowledge management. In 2011-12 the initial global program was expanded with a 5 year program to provide funding to multilateral partners including the World Bank Water and Sanitation Program (WSP); the World Health Organization Water Quality Partnership for Health (WHO WQP); the Water Supply and Sanitation Collaborative Council (WSSCC); the UNICEF/WHO Joint Monitoring Program (JMP); and the Sanitation and Water for All Partnership (SWA). Funding provided to the partnerships under WSI has increased the impact, effectiveness and responsiveness of wa

rows.title: Contracts Management - Contracts Levy 2009-10
rows.description: The Contracts Management Levy Administrative Support will support the provision of specialist services managed by Operations Policy and Support supplied directly to programs. The total value of this multi-country initiative is $6.5 million over 3 years, starting 2009-10.

rows.title: Core OPS Services 2009-10
rows.description: Funding under this initiative supports the delivery of the Operations Policy and Support Branch functions, specifically: administrative support; legal services including fees, reference material, legal training and conferences; strategic engagement; policy implementation; procurement and agreement services; and quality and performance management. The total value of this initiative is $1.2 million over 2 years, starting 2009-10.

rows.title: World Food Programme 2009-10 Assoc Costs
rows.description: This initiative supports the World Food Programme (WFP), under the Emergency Assistance Program Fund. This funding will be utilised by the WFP for operations and cost associated with the purchase, transportation and storage of commodities in response to emergency situations in countries that have been identified as priority developing countries by Australia. This funding also assists Australia meet its commitments under the Food Aid Convention. The total value of this multi-country initiative is $93 million over 2 years, starting 2008-09.

rows.title: BRIDGE Civic Education Phase 2
rows.description: The BRIDGE Civic Education Phase 2 initiative supports the 'Improving Responsiveness and Accountability in Government' budget measure to improve citizens' access to information. Funding under this initiative will provide support to people working in the field of civic education in the Asia Pacific region to improve efficiency and effectiveness of adult democracy and governance programs. The total value of this initiative is $1.2 million over 3 years, starting 2009-10.

rows.title: Direct Aid Program (DAP) 2009-10
rows.description: The Direct Aid Program (DAP) is a small grants scheme administered by the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT) through Australia's diplomatic posts in developing partner countries. The DAP's objective is to deliver targeted local level assistance. The total value for this multi-country initiative for the financial year 2009/10 is $7.5 million.

rows.title: UNCERF Multi-year Commitment
rows.description: Provision of financial support to the UN Central Emergency Response Fund (CERF) to enable more timely and reliable humanitarian assistance to those affected by natural disasters and armed conflict. The Fund aims to: promote early action and response to reduce loss of life; enhance response to time-critical requirements; and strengthen core elements of humanitarian response in underfunded crises. The total value of this initiative is $82.26 million over 5 years, starting 2009-10.

rows.title: Colombo Plan &#38; Drug Advisory Programme II
rows.description: The Colombo Plan for Cooperative Economic and Social Development in Asia and the Pacific (The Colombo Plan) is a regional inter-governmental organisation established to enhance economic and social development in the region. Australian assistance is provided through: core budget support; and a contribution to the Colombo Plan Drug Advisory Program (DAP). The total value of this initiative is $0.2 million over four years, starting 2009-10.

rows.title: ANCP Partnership Agreements
rows.description: The AusAID-Non-Government Organisations (NGOs) Cooperation Program (ANCP) is a long-running program that subsidises the overseas development activities of accredited Australian NGOs. Through the ANCP, AusAID supports accredited Australian NGOs to implement their own international development programs.  This initiative specifically provides funding to 'Partnership' NGOs. Under the Partnership agreements, AusAID and the partnership NGOs are working more strategically on addressing issues such as child protection, gender, food security and climate change to accelerate the joint efforts of both government and non-government organisations to help reach the Millennium Development Goals. The ANCP also aims to enhance NGO effectiveness and accountability through a well-developed accreditation and reporting system, and to promote innovation in the sector through the ANCP Innovations Fund.  The total value of the program for all countries is $213.4 million over 4 years, starting 2009-10.

rows.title: South Asia Water and Sanitation
rows.description: The South Asia Water and Sanitation initiative funds activities identified under the Water and Sanitation Initiative (WSI) in Bangladesh, Nepal, India and Sri Lanka to: expand access to sustainable and safe rural water supply and sanitation; support government reforms and build institutional capacity through providing policy advice and piloting initiatives; improve health through better hygiene practices and improved water quality; and support decentralization by channelling funds to programs that devolve decision-making to local government and communities. A particular focus will be adopted within activities to address the needs of the poor and other vulnerable groups, women and children. The total value of this multi-country initiative is $30.9 million over 4 years, starting 2009-10.

rows.title: Thematic Issues: Fragility and Conflict
rows.description: Funding under this initiative provides support for armed violence reduction, including conflict impacting on children and gender based conflict through a variety of activities: small arms survey; support to Papua New Guinea's attendance at associated meetings; support for gun policy web knowledge base; and approval for Timor Leste Armed Violence Assessment (TLAVA). The total value of this initiative is $0.7 million over 2 years, starting 2009-10.

rows.title: South Asia PSLP
rows.description: The South Asia Public Sector Linkages Program (PSLP) initiative provides funding to departments of Australian Commonwealth, State and Territory governments and Australian public universities. Use of the funds gives these selected organisations the opportunity to transfer capacity-building skills and expertise to their public sector counterpart institutions in South Asian partner countries. It will also support strengthening of sustainable development-focused public sector bilateral and regional linkages and improved governance and management of their public sectors. The total value of this multi-country initiative is $11.1 million over 5 years, starting 2009-10.

rows.title: SAARC Agricultural Research and Training Project
rows.description: The South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC) aims to improve water productivity in rain-fed and irrigated smallholder farming systems in South Asia to enhance agricultural productivity and food security. One of the most significant emerging threats to food security in South Asia is lack of water availability and the need for improved water productivity in both rain-fed and irrigated agriculture. Persistent over-utilisation of groundwater resources for irrigated agriculture has resulted in current water resources in the region being severely depleted. In addition, the performance of rain-fed agriculture in the region lags behind the environmental potential possible from modern farming practices. ACIAR and CSIRO will work in partnerships with in-country agricultural research agencies to study existing farming systems and develop improved approaches and technologies to enable farmers improve agricultural productivity through better management. New technologies may include

rows.title: Child Survival and Nutrition Initiative II
rows.description: The Child Survival and Nutrition Phase II initiative aims to contribute in reducing child mortality and enhance the nutritional status of children in Nepal, specifically support to the: National Vitamin A Program (NVAP); and Community Based Integrated Management of Childhood Illness (CBIMCI). The NVAP focuses predominantly on children aged 6 to 9 months through provision of complementary micronutrient products. The CBIMCI covers all 75 districts and provides interventions such as community based treatment of pneumonia and management of diarrhoea. The total value of this initiative is $0.8 million to be expensed over the 2009-10 financial year.

rows.title: Gender Capacity Building, Research and Advocacy
rows.description: Funding under this initiative supports capacity building, research and advocacy activities which will contribute to the scaling up of gender policy, with particular emphasis on women's leadership and economic empowerment. Funding will support: women's leadership program to generate key policy on the central role of leaders, elites and coalitions in indigenous institution and states and development of women's leadership case studies across Africa, the Middle East, Asia and the Pacific regions; sponsorship of 2011 International Women's Day activities; research on men's perspectives on the determinants and consequences of son preference and sex selection in Vietnam, Nepal and Pakistan; development of framework for future work program; and identification of research gaps in relation to women and the economy. The total value of this initiative is $0.6 million to be expensed during the 2009-10 financial year.

rows.title: Maternal Health Services for the Disadvantaged
rows.description: Reducing maternal mortality is one of the key priorities for the Government of Nepal's health sector program. Australia's support delivered through the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) aims to provide increased maternal health services to disadvantaged women in five rural district of Nepal - Rukum, Accham, Humla, Panchthar and Udaypur. UNICEF will provide both implementation and technical support to the Government of Nepal's Family Health Division (FHD) and District Public Health Offices from the five districts. The project will adopt a two pronged strategy: 1. strengthening demand creation at the community level by raising community awareness; and 2. strengthening services at the health facility level for improved utilisation of services, to ensure mothers and their newborn receive a continuum of care from antenatal (pre-delivery) to post partum (post delivery) period and from home to the health facility. The total value of this initiative is $1.0 million expensed during the 20

rows.title: PMU South 2000
rows.description: The activity does not have a description. You can contact Sida for more information. Call +46 8 698 50 00 or send an e-mail to sida@sida.se.

rows.title: Electoral Education and Information Centre
rows.description: Funding under this initiative provides support for Australia's commitment to the United Nations Development Program (UNDP) for the Establishment of  an Electoral Education and Information Centre  at the Election Commission in Kathmandu Nepal. The centre will increase community understanding of the electoral process and help entrench democracy in Nepal. Australia's contribution will be used to fund and manage the construction, equipment and the first year of operationalization of the centre to support Nepal's future electoral processes. The total value of this initiative is $1.3 million expensed in the 2009-10 financial year.

rows.title: Australia Awards
rows.description: Australia Awards are prestigious international scholarships and fellowships funded by the Australian Government offering the next generation of global leaders an opportunity to undertake study, research and professional development. Australia Awards target 67 countries in Asia, Pacific, Africa, the Caribbean and Latin America attracting high-achieving individuals from both public and private sectors. Funding under this initiative provides support for administrative costs of the Australian Awards Board which was created in November 2009 and establishment of the Australian Awards Secretariat. The Australia Awards Board advises the Australian Government on the Australia Awards Program, supporting the board is the role of the Australia Awards Secretariat. The total value of this initiative is $2.0 million dollars expensed during the 2009-10 financial year.

rows.title: Global Agriculture and Food Security Program
rows.description: The Global Agriculture and Food Security Program (GAFSP) is a multi-donor trust fund established by the World Bank to scale-up agricultural assistance to low-income countries. The focus of GAFSP is to improve the income and food security of poor people in developing countries through more and better country-led public and private sector investment by raising agricultural productivity; linking farmers to markets; reducing risk and vulnerability; improving non-farm rural livelihoods; and supporting technical assistance, institution building and capacity building. GAFSP will provide grants to developing countries and regional organisations that have demonstrated their commitment to a comprehensive approach for increasing agricultural growth and lasting improvements in food security. It will also include a private sector financing window providing loans and equity investments to stimulate private sector investment in agriculture and food security. The total value of this initiative is $100

rows.title: World Food Programme MRC &#38; SFP 2010-2012
rows.description: Funding under this initiative provides support to the World Food Programme (WFP) through the Multilateral Regular Contribution (MRC) and School Feeding Programme (SFP) as part of AusAID's commitment under the Australia WFP Strategic Partnership Agreement of 26 October 2009. The MRC will provide for food and associated costs, responding to sudden onset emergencies (either natural or man-made) and to food aid operations covering protracted recovery and relief support. The total value of this multi country initiative is $90 million over two years, starting 2010-11.

rows.title: Contracts Management Levy 2010-11
rows.description: The Contracts Management Levy directly supports procurement and contract management processes for delivery of aid programs.  It also funds legal services (clearances) for all tenders, contracts, grants and other forms of agreement with partner governments and multilateral organisations.  The total value of this multi-country initiative is $3.7 million over 2 years, starting 2010-11.

rows.title: Direct Aid Program (DAP) 2010-11
rows.description: The Direct Aid Program (DAP) is a small grants scheme administered by the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT) through Australia's diplomatic posts in developing partner countries. The DAP's objective is to deliver targeted local level assistance. The total value of this multi-country initiative for the financial year 2010-11 is $7.8 million.

rows.title: ALA Scholarships 2011 Intake
rows.description: The Australian Leadership Awards (ALA) aim to develop leadership and build partnerships and linkages within developing countries, while addressing priority development issues of the region.  ALA Scholarships are for study at Masters or Doctorate level in an Australian university. Study programs must relate to a priority development area: disability, economic growth, education, environment, food security, gender, governance, health, human rights, infrastructure, regional stability, rural development, water and sanitation. ALA scholars also undertake a Leadership Development Program in Australia.  In 2011, a total of 205 ALA scholarships will be awarded. The total value of this multi-country initiative is $35.0 million over 7 years, starting 2010-11.

rows.title: Enhancing Education - Scholarships 2011 Intake
rows.description: Enhancing Education aims to deepen and broaden Australia's current scholarship engagement to strengthen leadership and technical capacity of developing countries to drive their own development. Enhancing Education will result in a further 2400 long and short term scholarships, and 600 additional awards. The total value of this multi-country initiative is $60.9 million over 7 years, starting 2010-11.

rows.title: Armed Violence Reduction (AVR)
rows.description: Preventing instability in the Asia-Pacific region and globally is a foreign policy priority for the government. Australia has been a strong supporter of and signatory to the UN Programme of Action (UN PoA) to Prevent, Combat and Eradicate the Illicit Trade in Small Arms and Light Weapons in All its Aspects since its inception in 2001; and the Geneva Declaration on Armed Violence since its inception in 2006. The UN PoA outlines steps for countries to take to prevent the illicit trade in small arms and light weapons (SALW), while the Geneva Declaration commits signatories to strengthening efforts to integrate strategies for armed violence reduction and conflict prevention into national, regional and multilateral development plans and programs. This funding supports various activities  which aim to reduce armed violence through grants to multiple organisations with a goal of AVR under the guidelines of UN PoA and the Geneva Declaration. The total value of this multi-country initiative is

rows.title: Security Review Volunteer Program
rows.description: Funding under the Security Review Volunteer Program initiative will be spent on a review of security arrangements for the Australian Volunteers for International Development program aims to ensure appropriate levels of personal safety and security arrangements are in place for overseas volunteers funded by AusAID. The security review will: undertake an audit of current procedures and practices; provide a business case for any changes to security arrangements; and provide an agreed standard on new security arrangements. The total value of this initiative is $0.4 million over two years, starting 2010-11.

rows.title: RedR Australia Partnership 2010/11 - 2017/18
rows.description: Funding under this investment represents Australia's contribution to RedR Australia to provide emergency assistance to communities devastated by conflict or major natural disasters through the selection, training and provision of competent and effective personnel to humanitarian relief programs worldwide as a United Nations Standby Partner and to international and national non-government organisations.  The total value of this investment is $39.9 million over 8 years, starting 2010-11.

rows.title: Nepal Health Sector Program Phase II
rows.description: Funding under this initiative supports the assistance to Nepal Health Sector Program-Implementation plan II (NHSP IP 2). It is a continuation of AusAID assistance for Health in phase I (Initiative number INI 610).  Funding will support the Ministry of Health and Population of the Government of Nepal for NHSP 2, to improve the health and nutritional status of the Nepalese population, especially the poor and excluded. The total value of this investment is $20.8 million over 5 years, starting 2010-11.

rows.title: Human Rights Small Grants Scheme 2010-11
rows.description: Human Rights Small Grants Scheme 2010-11 provides small grants to non-government organisations and human rights institutions in developing countries for activities aimed at promoting and protecting human rights. In 2010-11, a total of 37 projects were funded across a multitude of countries. The total value of this initiative is $3.3 million, over 2 years, starting 2010-11.

rows.title: WFP Disaster Management Exercise Capability
rows.description: The World Food Programme's Readiness Initiative seeks to address the problem of increased natural disasters through proliferation of disaster management exercises as a tool in preparedness. Exercises will take place in the relevant country, use realistic scenarios and involve the same individuals and teams that will actually manage the response. The total value of this initiative is $4.4 million, starting 2010-11.

rows.title: IPPF South Asia Strategic Plan 2010-2015
rows.description: Funding under this initiative supports the International Planned Parenthood Federation (IPPF) South Asia Strategic Plan 2010-2015 and forms part of the Government's commitment to advance Millennium Development Goal (MDG) 5 (improve maternal health) in South Asia. The South Asia region has the highest rates of maternal and neonatal deaths and unmet demand for contraception in the Asia-Pacific. The funding of this initiative helps reduce maternal deaths in South Asia by improving equitable access for underserved groups to a range of reproductive health services and education including family planning. These goals are met by IPPF's South Asia Strategic Plan which focuses on 4 priority areas: ensuring young people have access and rights to services and education; reducing social, cultural and economic barriers that impede access; HIV/AIDS prevention and care; and reducing unsafe abortion. The total value of this multi-country initiative is up to $13.0 million over 4 years, starting 2010-11

rows.title: South Asia Food and Nutrition Security Initiative
rows.description: South Asia Food and Nutrition Security Initiative (SAFANSI) forms part of the Government's commitment to advance Millennium Development Goals (MDG) in South Asia: MDG 1C reduce hunger; MDG 4 reduce child deaths; and MDG 5 improve maternal health. Malnutrition's most devastating impact is on foetal growth, and during the first two years of a child's life. Malnutrition accounts for up to 40% of child deaths each year. Despite healthy levels of economic growth, many of South Asia's countries have persistent and unacceptable levels of malnutrition with complex causality. South Asia has both the highest rates and the largest numbers of malnutrition in the world. Gender inequality and social exclusion are at the heart of the malnutrition problem in South Asia. About 23% (336 million) of the population in South Asia are routinely hungry, without access to enough calories, every day. This is greatly undermining human development and limiting South Asia's productivity with potential GDP loss es

rows.title: Nepal Scaling Up Nutrition Initiative Tech Assist
rows.description: Funding under this proposal supports Australia's commitment to advance Millennium Development Goals (MDG) 1c to reduce hunger and MDG4 reduce child deaths and MDG5 to improve maternal health through the provision of core funding to support the Nepal Scaling Up Nutrition Initiative Technical Assistance (SUNITA) a World Bank administered trust fund. The total value of this initiative is $1.9 million over 3 years, starting 2010-11.

rows.title: Public Financial Management Initiative for Nepal
rows.description: The Public Financial Management Initiative for Nepal covers two activities namely 1) The Public Financial Management Multi Donor Trust Fund (MDTF) and 2) PFM Advisory Services for the Nepal Program. The Multi-Donor Trust Fund aims to strengthen the performance, transparency and accountability in public financial management in Nepal. Key results expected from the projects to be funded through the MDTF are: improved  resource management as a result of strengthened PFM systems and processes; increased awareness and oversight of government PFM processes by diverse constituencies; and better knowledge of value for money challenges and PFM governance gaps, contributing to improved design of PFM interventions at the country and sector level. The MDTF will be used as a vehicle to support a wide range of activities based on priorities set by the government and development partners broadly categorized under three broad dimensions:  Supply, Demand and Knowledge. MDTF will support activities that

rows.title: World Food Programme 2011-12 Emergency Assistance
rows.description: The World Food Programme (WFP) aims to  save lives in refugee and other emergency situations; to improve the nutrition and quality of life of the most vulnerable people at critical times in their lives; and  to help build assets and promote the self-reliance of poor people and communities, particularly through labour-intensive works programmes. Funding under this initiative supports the WFP's Emergency Assistance Program Fund covering the operations and cost associated with the purchase, transportation and storage of commodities in response to emergency situations in countries that have been identified as priority developing countries by Australia. The total value of this multi-country initiative is $25.0 million expensed during the 2010-11 financial year.

rows.title: Australian Development Awards - 2012 intake
rows.description: Funding under this initiative supports the Australian Development Awards long-term scholarships and short-term fellowships and scholarships 2012 intake of awardees from across 36 developing countries. Development Awards provide opportunities for people to gain knowledge and skills to assist in the development of their home country and are linked directly to outcomes of bilateral partnerships and country strategies. This funding covers all costs, including tuition fees and stipends for awardees while studying in Australia. The total value of this multi-country initiative is $301.3 million over nine years, starting 2011-12.
